content.text:
### Top X Posts on AI Security, Prompt Security, AI Optimization, System Prompts, and XML Prompts

Recent discussions on X (as of early February 2026) heavily focus on **AI agent security**, particularly vulnerabilities like **prompt injection attacks**, alongside emerging tools and partnerships. Here's a curated summary of the top 10 relevant posts (sorted by engagement and relevance):

#### Key Themes & Highlights:

1. **AI Agent Protection Tools**:
   - SolPay announced **Agent Immune System (AIS)**, a security layer for AI agents to prevent prompt injection, secret leaks, and runaway spending. Built for the Colosseum Agent Hackathon, it uses x402 micropayments on Solana.[[1]](https://x.com/i/status/2019143456145265091)[[2]](https://x.com/i/status/2019115480292962782)
   - Zypher Network partners with FISH for ZK-powered trustless AI agents, emphasizing **Proof of Prompt** for verifiable, secure on-chain AI behavior.[[3]](https://x.com/i/status/2018576960373641223)

2. **Vulnerabilities & Supply Chain Risks**:
   - Critical **Docker vulnerability (DockerDash)**: Indirect prompt injection leads to RCE and data leaks. Reported by Noma Security in collaboration with Docker's team.[[4]](https://x.com/i/status/2018698884814393480)[[5]](https://x.com/i/status/2018702065598370104)
   - Warnings about **autonomous AI agents** introducing prompt injection as a new attack surface in workflows like security ops and customer support.[[6]](https://x.com/i/status/2018275535697146247)

3. **Prompt Optimization & Broader AI Security**:
   - Pydantic's guide to automating **prompt optimization** using GEPA, Pydantic AI, and Evals—shifting from manual tweaks to scalable eval-driven improvements.[[7]](https://x.com/i/status/2018435053680779486)
   - A reminder that many "AI-specific" issues (e.g., prompt injection, model extraction) are **repackaged traditional security problems**.[[8]](https://x.com/i/status/2018968293541925129)

4. **Production AI Workflows**:
   - Kubernetes for GenAI scaling when security, audits, and agentic workflows (branching, tools, approvals) kick in.[[9]](https://x.com/i/status/2017708578392453211)
   - Video breakdown of **5 technical pillars** for production AI agents: data, security, and beyond "vibe coding" or simple prompts.[[10]](https://x.com/i/status/2017407633578463526)

No posts directly matched "XML prompts" or "system prompt" in isolation, but the security and optimization themes dominate. Conversations emphasize shifting from hype to robust, secure implementations. For deeper dives (e.g., threads or videos), let me know specifics!
